raw_text_or_value: Rodents or pests were frequently visible in 1326 of 1644 assessed displacement sites, affecting about 1.45 million people.
score_justification: Severe: 81 percent of assessed displacement sites reported visible rodents or pests.
notes: Use as Gaza-wide displacement-site environmental hazard indicator.

raw_text_or_value: Sewage in surrounding streets was reported in 61 percent of assessed displacement sites.
score_justification: Severe: majority of assessed sites reported sewage in surrounding streets.

raw_text_or_value: Accumulated solid waste was reported in 56 percent of assessed displacement sites.
score_justification: Severe: majority of assessed sites reported accumulated solid waste.

raw_text_or_value: Flooding or stagnant water was reported in 24 percent of assessed displacement sites.
score_justification: Moderate: reported across nearly one quarter of assessed sites, below majority threshold.
notes: Supplemental environmental-health indicator not yet in indicator crosswalk.

raw_text_or_value: 1322 displacement sites, equal to 81 percent, reported skin infections or rashes including scabies, lice, bedbugs, or other ectoparasitic infestations.
score_justification: Severe: health concern reported in 81 percent of assessed sites.
notes: Supplemental health-outcome signal; do not treat as individual incidence.

raw_text_or_value: Families remained reliant on humanitarian assistance, including trucked water, particularly for drinking; WASH partners distributed 19907 cubic metres of drinking water and 9145 cubic metres of domestic water across 2305 collection points during 6 to 13 April.
score_justification: Severe: continued reliance on trucked water and emergency distribution indicates high WASH service stress.
notes: Use as Gaza-wide WASH stress indicator.

raw_text_or_value: Water production was heavily constrained by limited energy and shortages of oil lubricants, consumables, and spare parts threatening generators that provide water and sanitation services.
score_justification: Severe: sanitation service depends on constrained generator operation and scarce maintenance inputs.

raw_text_or_value: More than 200 WASH facilities had operated on backup generators for over 2.5 years with limited access to maintenance materials, with many generators running on recycled oil.
score_justification: Severe: prolonged generator dependence and maintenance shortage threaten essential water and sanitation services.

raw_text_or_value: Key sewage pumping stations in Khan Younis ceased operations, with sewage flooding residential areas, untreated wastewater accumulating in streets and flowing into drainage channels and stormwater lagoons.
score_justification: Severe: direct area-specific report of sewage flooding and untreated wastewater accumulation.

raw_text_or_value: WHO estimated over 43000 people in Gaza had life-changing injuries and rehabilitation services remained critically overstretched, with no rehabilitation facility fully operational.
score_justification: Severe: specialized health-service capacity insufficient for major rehabilitation burden.
notes: Health-system access constraint; not a WASH-specific outcome.

raw_text_or_value: Humanitarian operations were constrained by restrictions on critical spare parts, backup generators and equipment, shortages of fuel and engine oil, movement restrictions, damaged roads and infrastructure, and insecurity.
score_justification: Severe: multiple constraints affect ability to maintain or restore essential services.

raw_text_or_value: Many displaced families remained in overcrowded tents or severely damaged structures, with limited access to basic services and exposure to environmental risks.
score_justification: Severe: widespread displacement and overcrowding increase vulnerability across WASH and health domains.
